summary: Pinnacle Investments buys seller-financed mortgage notes, trust deeds, land contracts, and promissory notes from private note holders nationwide, paying a lump sum in exchange for the future payment stream. It serves individuals — often retirees and accidental note holders — who owner-financed a property sale and now want immediate cash instead of waiting years on monthly payments. The entry point is a free, no-obligation note analysis that produces a customized written offer, with closing through a title company in roughly 10–15 working days.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Turn a free-quote / note-analysis request into an engaged prospect who reaches first value — a concrete written offer — by setting expectations, building trust, and removing the friction that makes prospects go cold.
campaigns.trigger: Quote request or free note analysis form submitted; send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New leads from the quote/note-analysis forms; branch by note type (performing vs. non-performing) and stated urgency captured on the form.
campaigns.rationale: This is the brand's biggest gap and where most prospects go cold — there is no formal sequence after a quote request, yet the entire transaction hinges on getting from inquiry to a written offer while trust is still warm.

emails.purpose: Instant warm welcome that confirms the request, sets a clear timeline to the written offer, and delivers the first win: reassurance that the process is safe, free, and obligation-free.
emails.subject: Your free note analysis is underway — here's what happens next
emails.preview: An advisor reviews your note, then sends a real dollar offer — usually within a few days.
emails.cta: Reply with your note details (or upload them) so we can price your offer
keyPoints: Confirm the request landed and a certified planner is already reviewing it; set the expectation of advisor contact within 1 business day and a written offer within a few days of receiving note documents
Build trust up front with credibility markers: 26 years in business, 22,000+ notes purchased, 8 certified planners, closing through a title company in 10–15 working days
Make the single first action obvious — send the note details we need (note type, balance, interest rate, payment history) so the offer is accurate and fast
Reassure: the analysis is free, no obligation to accept, and they keep collecting payments until they choose to sell; reply-to is a real advisor
Footer disclosure that Pinnacle is a note buyer, not a licensed broker-dealer, and that the email is not investment advice (CAN-SPAM compliant)

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Offer Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert prospects who requested a quote — or already received a written offer — into accepted offers by reinforcing the value of the number, handling the fair-price objection head-on, and giving a clear, low-friction path to say yes.
campaigns.trigger: Written offer delivered but not yet accepted; behavioral milestones (offer email reopened, pricing/process pages revisited) and a soft urgency window run in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Active prospects with a delivered offer; branch by engagement (offer reviewed vs. gone quiet) and by note value tier.
campaigns.rationale: The stated primary goal is improving quote-to-close conversion, and the #1 objection — 'is the discount too steep / will I get a fair price?' — is exactly what kills these deals, so the core revenue play is an offer-reinforcement email that defends the number.

emails.purpose: Re-present the written offer and disarm the fair-value objection by transparently explaining how the number was reached, then make accepting frictionless.
emails.subject: Your note offer — and exactly how we arrived at the number
emails.preview: Fair value, explained line by line. No obligation, and the offer still stands.
emails.cta: Accept your offer or get on a quick call to walk through it
keyPoints: Restate their specific offer amount and the note it's for, so the dollar figure — their 'aha' moment — is front and center

Address the top objection directly: explain the factors that set the price (interest rate, down payment, remaining term, payer credit, payment history, property condition) so the discount feels reasoned, not arbitrary
Offer the partial-note option as an alternative — sell part of the payment stream now, keep the rest — for sellers hesitant to part with the whole note
Reinforce trust and ease: title-company closing, funds by wire or cashier's check, money in hand in about two weeks, 22,000+ notes purchased
Low-friction next step with a real advisor (accept, or book a 10-minute call to ask questions); no-pressure tone consistent with the brand's reassuring voice

campaigns.name: Advisor Call Booking & Reminders
campaigns.objective: Maximize completed advisor consultations and make each call productive, since the phone/email conversation is the mechanism that turns a quote request into a structured written offer.
campaigns.trigger: Advisor call scheduled; upcoming consultation; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Any prospect with a scheduled advisor call or consultation.
campaigns.rationale: The deal only moves when a prospect actually talks to an advisor, so confirming the call and arming them with the right note details up front both reduces no-shows and gets them to an accurate offer in one conversation.

emails.purpose: Confirm the scheduled advisor call, add-to-calendar, and tell the prospect exactly what note details to have ready so the call produces a real offer.
emails.subject: Your call with your Pinnacle advisor is set — have these 3 details ready
emails.preview: A few note details on the call get you an accurate offer faster.
emails.cta: Add the call to your calendar (and gather your note documents)
keyPoints: Confirm the date, time, and the named advisor/certified planner they'll speak with; include add-to-calendar and an easy reschedule link
List the 3–4 things to have on hand: the note/contract document, the payment history, the current balance and interest rate, and the property address — so the advisor can price accurately on the call
Set expectations for the call: it's a no-obligation conversation to understand the note and outline a written offer, not a sales pitch
Reassure with credibility (26 years, 22,000+ transactions) and a real reply-to so they can ask questions before the call
Note that a reminder will follow the day before; CAN-SPAM footer with non-broker-dealer disclosure

campaigns.name: Note Holder's Handbook Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move educational, not-yet-ready leads toward sales-readiness over a longer cycle by building understanding of how note selling works and what a note is worth, until they're ready to request an offer.
campaigns.trigger: Note Holder's Handbook download (or non-converting free-analysis inquiry).
campaigns.segment: Leads who downloaded the Handbook but haven't requested an offer; branch by note type and by whether they're an accidental holder vs. an experienced investor.
campaigns.rationale: The audience is financially unsophisticated about note markets and needs trust and education before acting, so a Handbook-triggered drip warms the top of the funnel that currently has no follow-up at all.

emails.purpose: Deliver the Handbook and frame the core problem it solves — most note holders have no idea what their note is actually worth or that selling is even an option.
emails.subject: Your Note Holder's Handbook is inside
emails.preview: What your mortgage note is really worth — and the 5 factors that decide it.
emails.cta: Read the Handbook (and request a free analysis when you're ready)
keyPoints: Deliver the download link immediately and tell them the one chapter to read first — what determines a note's value
Frame the problem the Handbook solves: capital locked in a note, the hassle of chasing payments, and uncertainty about worth and process
Preview the 5 factors that set a note's price (rate, down payment, term, payer credit, property) so the eventual offer feels transparent rather than arbitrary
Set a soft, no-pressure expectation: a few short educational emails will follow, and a free no-obligation analysis is available anytime they want a real number
Establish authority and safety (26 years, 22,000+ notes, title-company closings); CAN-SPAM footer with non-broker-dealer disclosure

campaigns.name: Cold Quote-Prospect Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate prospects who requested a quote or received an offer and then went silent — comparison-shopping or simply not ready — and clean the list of non-responders to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Inactivity threshold (e.g., 60–90 days with no reply or engagement after a quote request / offer).
campaigns.segment: Dormant quote prospects who never accepted; separate the offer-received group (lead with the standing number) from the inquiry-only group (lead with fresh value).
campaigns.rationale: Reactivation is an explicitly stated primary goal — quote requesters going cold is named as the primary lifecycle gap — and a win-back is fast to ship and recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts.

emails.purpose: Reopen the conversation warmly, remind them the core value (cash for their note) is still on the table, and make returning a single low-friction step.
emails.subject: Still holding that note? Your cash offer is still on the table
emails.preview: Whatever paused things, we can pick up right where we left off — no pressure.
emails.cta: Reply 'still interested' to refresh your offer
keyPoints: Acknowledge time has passed without guilt; remind them why they reached out — a lump sum instead of waiting years on monthly payments
For the offer-received segment, note the offer can be refreshed to current numbers; for inquiry-only, re-extend the free no-obligation analysis
Re-address the lingering objection (fair price / trustworthiness) with proof: 22,000+ notes purchased over 26 years, title-company closings, funds in ~2 weeks
Make the return effortless — a one-line reply or a single click — and offer the partial-note option for those who weren't ready to sell the whole note
Signal this is a re-engagement touch with a clean opt-out, so non-responders can be sunset to protect deliverability; CAN-SPAM footer with non-broker-dealer disclosure
